title: Self-Improving Agent — CIFAR-10 CNN の自動改善ループ
summary: Claude が train.py を書き換える → 訓練 → 評価 → 失敗から学習する 24/365 ループ。5 サイクルで最高精度 0.6957 を達成。

improvement.description: Add StepLR learning rate scheduler to reduce learning rate every 2 epochs
improvement.reasoning: Learning rate scheduling is a proven technique that helps models converge better. Starting with a higher learning rate for faster initial learning, then reducing it allows for fine-tuning in later epochs. This should help achieve higher accuracy without increasing training time significantly.

cycles.diff_excerpt:
--- train.py.prev
+++ train.py
@@ -107,6 +107,9 @@
     model = SimpleCNN().to(DEVICE)
     criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
     optimizer = optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=LEARNING_RATE, momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4)
+    
+    # Add learning rate scheduler
+    scheduler = optim.lr_scheduler.StepLR(optimizer, step_size=2, gamma=0.7)
 
     start_time = time.time()
 
@@ -125,7 +128,11 @@
             running_loss += loss.item()
 
         avg_loss = running_loss / len(trainloader)
-        print(f"Epoch {epoch + 1}/{EPOCHS}, Loss: {avg_loss:.4f}", flush=True)
+        current_lr = optimizer.param_groups[0]['lr']
+        print(f"Epoch {epoch + 1}/{EPOCHS}, Loss: {avg_loss:.4f}, LR: {current_lr:.6f}", flush=True)
+        
+        # Step the scheduler
+        scheduler.step()
 
     training_time = time.time() - start_time

improvement.description: Add dropout regularization to fully connected layers
improvement.reasoning: The current model has no dropout regularization, which is crucial for preventing overfitting in neural networks. Adding dropout with p=0.5 to the fully connected layers will force the model to learn more robust features and typically improves test accuracy by 2-5%. This is a standard practice in CNN architectures.

cycles.diff_excerpt:
--- train.py.prev
+++ train.py
@@ -21,7 +21,7 @@
 # ============================================================================
 
 class SimpleCNN(nn.Module):
-    """CNN with batch normalization for improved performance."""
+    """CNN with batch normalization and dropout for improved performance."""
 
     def __init__(self):
         super().__init__()
@@ -32,6 +32,7 @@
         self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
         self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
         self.fc1 = nn.Linear(32 * 8 * 8, 128)
+        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(0.5)
         self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, 10)
         self.relu = nn.ReLU()
 
@@ -40,6 +41,7 @@
         x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn2(self.conv2(x))))
         x = x.view(-1, 32 * 8 * 8)
         x = self.relu(self.fc1(x))
+        x = self.dropout(x)
         x = self.fc2(x)
         return x

improvement.description: Add a third convolutional layer with 64 filters and batch normalization
improvement.reasoning: The current model has only 2 conv layers with 16 and 32 filters, which may be insufficient for CIFAR-10's complexity. Adding a third layer with 64 filters will increase the model's capacity to learn more complex features and representations, potentially improving accuracy. This preserves all existing improvements while expanding the architecture.

cycles.diff_excerpt:
--- train.py.prev
+++ train.py
@@ -30,8 +30,10 @@
         self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(16)
         self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(16, 32, 3, padding=1)
         self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
+        self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
+        self.bn3 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
         self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
-        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(32 * 8 * 8, 128)
+        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(64 * 4 * 4, 128)
         self.dropout = nn.Dropout(0.5)
         self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, 10)
         self.relu = nn.ReLU()
@@ -39,7 +41,8 @@
     def forward(self, x):
         x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x))))
         x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn2(self.conv2(x))))
-        x = x.view(-1, 32 * 8 * 8)
+        x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn3(self.conv3(x))))
+        x = x.view(-1, 64 * 4 * 4)
         x = self.relu(self.fc1(x))
         x = self.dropout(x)
         x = self.fc2(x)

improvement.description: Replace BatchNorm with GroupNorm for better regularization and stability
improvement.reasoning: GroupNorm can provide better regularization than BatchNorm, especially with moderate batch sizes. It's less dependent on batch statistics and can help with generalization. This change maintains all existing improvements while potentially improving feature learning.

cycles.diff_excerpt:
--- train.py.prev
+++ train.py
@@ -21,17 +21,17 @@
 # ============================================================================
 
 class SimpleCNN(nn.Module):
-    """CNN with batch normalization and dropout for improved performance."""
+    """CNN with group normalization and dropout for improved performance."""
 
     def __init__(self):
         super().__init__()
-        # Conv layers with batch normalization
+        # Conv layers with group normalization
         self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 16, 3, padding=1)
-        self.bn1 = nn.BatchNorm2d(16)
+        self.gn1 = nn.GroupNorm(4, 16)  # 4 groups for 16 channels
         self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(16, 32, 3, padding=1)
-        self.bn2 = nn.BatchNorm2d(32)
+        self.gn2 = nn.GroupNorm(8, 32)  # 8 groups for 32 channels
         self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
-        self.bn3 = nn.BatchNorm2d(64)
+        self.gn3 = nn.GroupNorm(16, 64)  # 16 groups for 64 channels
         self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
         self.fc1 = nn.Linear(64 * 4 * 4, 128)
         self.dropout = nn.Dropout(0.5)
@@ -39,9 +39,9 @@
         self.relu = nn.ReLU()
 
     def forward(self, x):
-        x = self.pool(self.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x))))

improvement.description: Add a fourth convolutional layer with 128 filters to increase model capacity for better feature extraction
improvement.reasoning: The current 3-layer CNN may be underfitting the complex CIFAR-10 dataset. Adding a fourth conv layer with 128 filters will increase the model's capacity to learn more sophisticated features and patterns, potentially improving accuracy. This follows the common practice of gradually increasing filter counts in deeper layers.

cycles.diff_excerpt:
--- train.py.prev
+++ train.py
@@ -32,8 +32,10 @@
         self.gn2 = nn.GroupNorm(8, 32)  # 8 groups for 32 channels
         self.conv3 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, 3, padding=1)
         self.gn3 = nn.GroupNorm(16, 64)  # 16 groups for 64 channels
+        self.conv4 = nn.Conv2d(64, 128, 3, padding=1)
+        self.gn4 = nn.GroupNorm(32, 128)  # 32 groups for 128 channels
         self.pool = nn.MaxPool2d(2, 2)
-        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(64 * 4 * 4, 128)
+        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(128 * 2 * 2, 128)
         self.dropout = nn.Dropout(0.5)
         self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, 10)
         self.relu = nn.ReLU()
@@ -42,7 +44,8 @@
         x = self.pool(self.relu(self.gn1(self.conv1(x))))
         x = self.pool(self.relu(self.gn2(self.conv2(x))))
         x = self.pool(self.relu(self.gn3(self.conv3(x))))
-        x = x.view(-1, 64 * 4 * 4)
+        x = self.pool(self.relu(self.gn4(self.conv4(x))))
+        x = x.view(-1, 128 * 2 * 2)
         x = self.relu(self.fc1(x))
         x = self.dropout(x)
         x = self.fc2(x)

note: 毎サイクル改善するわけではない。退行（サイクル3、5）は、ロールバックと失敗履歴管理（NRR）の必要性を体現している。
